business.headline: Air India reports $2.8 billion loss in FY26, biggest since Tata takeoverAccording to Singapore Airlines' annual report, Air India's losses stood at 3.56 billion Singapore dollars during the financial year.

Paytm's pivotal year: How Vijay Shekhar Sharma steered Indian fintech's boldest turnaround storyPaytm reported its first full-year profit of Rs 552 crore in FY26, capping a sharp reset under Vijay Shekhar Sharma. The numbers shift attention from last year's regulatory shock to cost discipline, UPI growth and an AI-led strategy.

Your office commute is getting costlier. Is 'work from home' the answer?Getting to office is starting to feel expensive. With fuel price hike concerns growing and salaries already stretched by inflation and EMIs, employees are increasingly asking whether work from home deserves a second look.

China shows interest in US oil after Trump-Xi talks on Iran and Strait of HormuzAccording to a White House readout, Trump and Xi agreed that the Strait of Hormuz should be reopened to support global energy needs. The waterway has effectively remained closed since the start of the US and Israel conflict with Iran.

Sensex ends 790 points higher, Nifty above 23,600; IT stocks tumbleThe S&P BSE Sensex surged 789.74 points, or 1.06%, to close at 75,398.72, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 277 points, or 1.18%, to settle at 23,689.60.

8th Pay Commission: Why government employees want DA merged into basic payEmployee unions have requested the 8th Pay Commission to absorb Dearness Allowance (DA) into basic pay. The move could alter salary calculations, allowances, pensions and retirement benefits for lakhs of employees.

Bedridden, but asked to visit bank for re-KYC? Why this keeps happening in IndiaMany customers complain that despite digital infrastructure existing on paper, some branches still insist on physical presence, hard-copy paperwork, or repeated document submissions.

8th Pay Commission update: Employee body pushes for 50% guaranteed pension and moreAs discussions about the 8th Pay Commission continue, a key employee body has put forward a set of demands aimed at improving retirement security. At the centre of it is a proposal for an assured pension under the National Pension System (NPS).

India seeks extension of US waiver on Russian oil amid Iran conflict: ReportThe waiver, initially approved by the US in March and later expanded through an authorisation valid until May 16, allowed Indian refiners to continue receiving already-loaded Russian cargoes.

Why PM is asking Indians to cut spending: NITI Aayog explains austerity messageAs tensions in West Asia continue to rise, Prime Minister Narendra Modi has urged Indians to be careful with spending and avoid unnecessary expenses. But why now? Is this simply a precautionary step or a sign of deeper economic worries? NITI Aayog Vice Chairman Ashok Lahiri explains what the Prime Minister's austerity message really means.

Damage is now evident: Market strategist Akshay Chinchalkar on oil, gold, rupeeIn an interaction with IndiaToday.in, Akshay Chinchalkar, Managing Partner and Head of Markets Strategy at The Wealth Company, explains rising market stress, rupee weakness, soaring gold prices and investor fears.

If not physical gold, then what? Why experts are backing ETFs as an alternativeWith gold prices surging after the import duty hike, investors are increasingly turning towards ETFs as a cheaper, more flexible alternative to physical bullion amid rising uncertainty and rupee weakness.

Why Trump took Musk, Tim Cook, Jensen Huang, other top CEOs to ChinaTravelling with US President Trump are some of the world's biggest corporate names, including Elon Musk, Jensen Huang, Tim Cook and Larry Fink, along with executives from companies such as Meta, Visa, Goldman Sachs, Boeing and Mastercard.

Why did India ban sugar exports? Here's what's happeningThe ban applies to exports of raw, white and refined sugar, although shipments already in the export pipeline under specific conditions will still be allowed. Certain quota-based exports to the US and European Union remain exempt.
Sensex jumps over 300 points, Nifty above 23,500; Airtel up 2%The S&P BSE Sensex jumped 391.36 points to 75,000.34, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 148.65 points to 23,561.25 as of 9:36 am.

Sensex, Nifty today: Will stock market crash or open high today?Dalal Street is set for a higher opening after a mild rebound, with GIFT Nifty pointing above Wednesday's close. The recovery remains tentative as a record-low rupee, high crude prices, persistent foreign selling and elevated volatility keep sentiment fragile.

Gold gets costlier after PM Modi's appeal. Is petrol, diesel next?After the government raised gold import duty, fresh attention has turned to petrol and diesel prices as crude stays high. Remarks by PM Narendra Modi, the RBI and the IMF have sharpened concerns that consumers may not stay protected indefinitely.

Govt bans sugar exports till September to cool domestic prices, risks global crisisThe decision comes as authorities try to prevent a rise in domestic sugar prices at a time when food inflation remains politically and economically sensitive for Indian households.

Rupee depreciation matter of concern, but no need to get alarmed about it: Ex-RBI chiefIn an exclusive interview with India Today, former Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Governor Duvvuri Subbarao said that the rupee's depreciation is a matter of concern, but there is no need to get alarmed about it.

From 4-day work week to higher PF: 11 big changes under the new labour codeIndia's new labour code could change how millions work and get paid, with possible changes to office hours, overtime rules, salary structure and gratuity benefits. Here are 11 important changes employees should know.

entertainment.headline: Athiradi review: Basil and Tovino's film is imperfect, but full of heart and humourRelease Date:Thu May 14, 2026 Arun Anirudhan's Athiradi follows Samkutty as he joins BCET College to revive Arohan after a deadly stampede shut the fest down. The film draws its strength from campus nostalgia, humour and the bond between two brothers, despite uneven writing elsewhere.

Suriya fans protest in Puducherry as Karuppu release faces last-minute delay Suriya fans in Puducherry protested outside theatres after several Karuppu shows were cancelled on release day. The disruption turned planned first-day celebrations into confusion as the film's release was pushed to May 15.

Suriya's Karuppu not releasing today, team targetting May 15: Theatre owner Suriya's Karuppu did not release on May 14, with theatres cancelling all shows and issuing refunds. The delay, linked to reported financial issues, has pushed the team to seek distributor clearance for May 15.

Prime Video, JioHotstar this week: Exam, Inspector Avinash S2, Nemesis and more OTT platforms are bringing a fresh lineup of thrillers, crime dramas and emotional stories this week, including Exam, Inspector Avinash: Season 2 and Nemesis. From socially relevant narratives to gripping cop dramas and glamorous fashion-world stories, viewers have plenty to add to their watchlist.

We meet again: Matt Reeves unveils The Batman: Part II cast in a unique reveal Matt Reeves shared the cast of The Batman: Part II through a series of social media posts in a one-of-a-kind reveal. He paired each post with a short message addressed to the returning actors' characters.

With you: Dulquer Salmaan, Vignesh Shivan support RJ Balaji amid Karuppu delay Actors and filmmakers Dulquer Salmaan, Vignesh Shivan and others posted messages backing RJ Balaji after Karuppu shows were cancelled on release day. The support came as reports linked the delay to unsettled dues.

Calm before the storm: NTRNeel new still released ahead of first glimpse. See here A new still of NTRNeel was unveiled on May 14 ahead of its first glimpse, set to be released on May 19. The film marks the first collaboration between Jr NTR and Prashanth Neel.

Indian Institute of Zombies review: A potentially fun comedy lost in translationRelease Date:Fri May 15, 2026 Indian Institute of Zombies follows a campus immortality experiment that unleashes a zombie outbreak. The film's promising satire and genre ambition are undercut by weak writing and tonal confusion.

Are you done now?: Visibly irked Alia Bhatt reacts as she gets mobbed at Cannes Alia Bhatt appeared visibly upset at the Bharat Pavilion in Cannes after fans and photographers crowded around her. The viral video has emerged as a major talking point from her latest festival outing.

Netflix this week: Kartavya, Desi Bling, Soul Mate and more Netflix is bringing a fresh lineup of releases this week, including Saif Ali Khan's action drama Kartavya and the glamorous reality-style series Desi Bling. From crime thrillers and emotional dramas to fantasy adventures, the platform has something for every kind of viewer.

Taylor Swift and Sabrina Carpenter win top songwriting honour at BMI Awards Singers Taylor Swift and Sabrina Carpenter shared one of the biggest honours at the 2026 BMI Pop Awards, alongside major wins for other top names in music.

Aishwarya Rai's Cannes poster absence was bound to sting. Outrage over it is valid Aishwarya Rai Bachchan's absence from L'Oreal's Cannes banners has triggered outrage on the internet. And while it may look like a case of just a face missing from the promotional materials, it is far more layered and deeply emotional. Because, Aishwarya at Cannes is not just red-carpet fashion for India.

Karuppu director breaks down over delay, hints at 6 pm release for Suriya's film Director-actor RJ Balaji apologised in a tearful video after Karuppu's morning and afternoon shows were cancelled on release day. The delay put the focus on reported unpaid dues and the team's push for an evening release.

Dan Levy's Big Mistakes renewed for Season 2 at Netflix Netflix has renewed Dan Levy's comedy Big Mistakes for a second season and will move him to a first-look TV deal. The first season, also starring Taylor Ortega, premiered on April 9.

Aditi Rao Hydari shares 'calm before Cannes' moments from hotel room Actor Aditi Rao Hydari shared pictures from her hotel room as she prepared for her appearance at the Cannes Film Festival. The post came as India's presence at the festival stayed in focus, with Alia Bhatt wrapping up her engagements.

Suriya's Karuppu releases in Pune amid delay in South states, videos go viral Suriya's Karuppu released in Pune after its morning shows were cancelled across several southern states over unpaid dues. The patchy rollout has put focus on the film's pending payments.

Bridgerton Season 5 production begins; Francesca-Michaela's love story set for 2027 Bridgerton Season 5 will return in 2027 with Season 5, ending its two-year release pattern. The new chapter centres on Francesca Bridgerton and Michaela Stirling as production begins in the UK.

Krishnavataram director Hardik Gajjar says he was wary of portraying Raas Leela Krishnavataram director Hardik Gajjar said portraying Radha-Krishna's Raas Leela was his toughest task. The sequence is now drawing strong audience response for balancing romance with dignity.

KPop Demon Hunters brings Huntr/x to life with new world concert tour Netflix and AEG Presents have announced a global concert tour for KPop Demon Hunters. Here's what is known so far about the live shows, the film's success and why fans are already waiting for tickets.

general news.headline: 2-day WFH, no-car day, online meets: Delhi's move after PM's save fuel appealIn step with the PM's save fuel appeal, the Delhi government announced that it will observe a "Metro Day", and also appeal to people to follow a "No Car Day" once every week.

Indian cargo ship sinks after drone strike near Oman, Delhi calls attack deplorableCargo ship Haji Ali was passing near Limah, off Oman's northern coast, at around 3:30 am on Wednesday when it was struck by an unidentified explosive object, suspected to be a drone or missile.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi in India colours as BCCI names squad for tri-seriesThe Indian Cricket Board has named the India A squad for tri-series against Sri Lanka A and Afghanistan A. Tilak Varma has been named as the captain of the 15-member squad.

Rejuvenation of China, MAGA can go hand in hand, host Xi tells Trump  Live UpdatesThe Chinese leader repeatedly stressed cooperation over confrontation, saying both countries stand to gain from cooperation and lose from confrontation.

MLAs favoured KC Venugopal, yet he was passed over for Kerala CM. Here's whyEven as most Congress MLAs reportedly rallied behind KC Venugopal, the party leadership ultimately chose the leader it believed best captured Kerala's public mood.

The NEET-UG whistleblower who reached Sikar police station in the dead of nightA whistleblower alerted the NTA about the question paper leak, which led to the cancellation of the NEET-UG 2026 exam. The exam for medical aspirants was held across India on May 3. At 1.30 am on May 4, the whistleblower went to a police station in Sikar with the guess papers as evidence, but left abruptly. This is what happened.

SIR gets kicking: Bengal, Bihar to bar deleted names from social welfare schemesThe Election Commission-led SIR exercise has now expanded beyond polls to plugging holes in direct benefit transfers. West Bengal and Bihar, two BJP-led states, will be using SIR data to identify duplicate, deceased and ineligible beneficiaries to plug leakages in welfare delivery systems.

What happened to Prateek Yadav? Doctor, autopsy reveal hidden health battlePrateek Yadav's doctor said he left the ICU against medical advice despite repeated warnings. His postmortem later attributed his death to massive pulmonary thromboembolism, ruling out steroid speculation.

Hyderabad Visa Temple on firing line as US Senator attacks H-1B fraudHyderabad's Chilkur Balaji Temple, long visited by devotees seeking H-1B visas, came under attack after a US senator launched a sharp tirade against work visas, alleging that they were being used to replace American workers with cheaper foreign labour.

Live: Trump calls Xi a friend, he says China and US should be partners, not rivalsTrump in China Live Updates: Donald Trump received a ceremonial welcome in Beijing as he began a two-day summit with Xi Jinping, marking a closely watched round of US-China talks. At the Great Hall of the People, Trump called it the "biggest summit ever" and highlighted his personal rapport with Xi, saying it was an honour to meet his "friend" and expressing confidence ties between the two nations would improve significantly. Xi said the shared interests of China and the United States outweigh their differences, adding that stable relations between the two powers benefit the wider world. The leaders stood together for national anthems, inspected a military honour guard and greeted children waving flags during the official ceremony. Trump's delegation included Elon Musk and Jensen Huang, as talks are expected to focus on trade access, Taiwan, advanced chip sales, and the regional fallout from the Iran conflict, including the Strait of Hormuz. Stay tuned to IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

What was the thundersquall that killed at least 89 in UP? Science explainedA violent thundersquall killed at least 89 people across five districts of Uttar Pradesh on May 13, collapsing homes and uprooting trees in Bhadohi, Fatehpur, and Budaun.

Tamil Nadu CM Vijay hikes DA by 2% for government employees, teachersVijay also ensured that his administration would be a 'government of common people' and they will work hard to ensure a situation of 'social justice, equal opportunity and secularism' in the state.

Vikas Khanna, Ranveer Brar and now Harsh Goenka wowed by Odia flavoursOdia cuisine is one of the most underrated, but with time, it is slowly getting its due. The most recent acknowledgement came from industrialist Harsh Goenka.

Live: No foreign trips for a year for Delhi ministers in step with PM's appealPM's forex-saving push Live Updates: Amid a global oil supply crunch and rising fuel prices, Prime Minister Narendra Modi has urged citizens and government departments to cut unnecessary expenditure and focus on savings. Stressing the need for economic resilience during a period of energy uncertainty, the Prime Minister called for prudent fuel consumption and the efficient use of resources to reduce the impact of the ongoing oil crisis on households and the broader economy. Following this, state governments and multiple departments have begun chalking out directives in this direction. Stay tuned to India Today as we bring you the latest developments.

Mamata Banerjee, in lawyer's gown, at court to argue post-poll violence caseThe case pertains to allegations of attacks on political workers and party offices following the high-stakes elections that ended the Trinamool Congress's 15-year rule and brought the BJP to power in the state.

China shows interest in US oil after Trump-Xi talks on Iran and Strait of HormuzAccording to a White House readout, Trump and Xi agreed that the Strait of Hormuz should be reopened to support global energy needs. The waterway has effectively remained closed since the start of the US and Israel conflict with Iran.

Fame Gurukul nostalgia hits hard as Arijit Singh-Qazi Touqeer reunite in JiaganjSinger Qazi Touqeer shared a video revisiting his journey with Arijit Singh, from their Fame Gurukul days to a recent visit to Jiaganj. The clip captures moments from scooty rides around the town to a friendly game of cricket, offering a nostalgic glimpse into their bond.

Mission 'release Jana Nayagan' next? CM Vijay asks producer to return from CannesAfter taking office as Tamil Nadu Chief Minister, Joseph Vijay has reopened efforts to secure clearance for Jana Nayagan and asked producer Venkat to return to Chennai. The move puts his stalled farewell film back in focus.

Suriya's Karuppu not releasing today, team targetting May 15: Theatre ownerSuriya's Karuppu did not release on May 14, with theatres cancelling all shows and issuing refunds. The delay, linked to reported financial issues, has pushed the team to seek distributor clearance for May 15.

Woman gang-raped inside bus in Delhi; driver, conductor arrestedA woman has alleged that a sleeper bus driver and conductor raped her inside the vehicle in Delhi. Police sources said the incident was reported to have taken place on May 12 and both men were arrested.

And while it may look like a case of just a face missing from the promotional materials, it is far more layered and deeply emotional. Because, Aishwarya at Cannes is not just red-carpet fashion for India.

Your office commute is getting costlier. Is 'work from home' the answer?Getting to office is starting to feel expensive. With fuel price hike concerns growing and salaries already stretched by inflation and EMIs, employees are increasingly asking whether work from home deserves a second look.

Pune woman receives late-night texts from restaurant staff, management fires himA Pune woman said that a restaurant employee accessed her phone number through a QR-code ordering system and sent her late-night messages. The employee was later terminated.

sports.headline: Drug ban setback to IPL resurgence: Kagiso Rabada recounts emotional 12 months After going through the lows of a drugs ban and the highs of winning an ICC title with South Africa, Kagiso Rabada said the difficult year taught him not to care too much about criticism and outside opinions.

PBKS vs MI LIVE: Punjab Kings look to arrest slump in playoffs race PBKS (Punjab) vs MI (Mumbai) Live Score, IPL 2026: Shreyas Iyer's Punjab Kings have lost four matches on the trot, and they will be looking to bounce back against the Mumbai Indians, who are already out of the tournament.

Italian Open match halted as Inter Milan's fireworks celebration sweeps Rome arena Italian Open 2026: The quarterfinal clash between Luciano Darderi and Rafael Jodar was temporarily halted after smoke from Inter Milan's Coppa Italia celebrations drifted into Rome's Foro Italico and disrupted visibility.

CSK sign South African all-rounder as replacement for injured Jamie Overton Chennai Super Kings have signed South African all-rounder Dian Forrester as a replacement for injured England all-rounder Jamie Overton for the remainder of the Indian Premier League 2026 season.

India won't play in FIFA World Cup 2026, but 3 Indian-origin players just might India may not be on the plane to the 2026 World Cup, but the tricolour connection is far from broken. From a 19-year-old sensation from Kerala to a former Bayern Munich prodigy, three Indian-origin footballers could make history on the world stage. As global heavyweights finalise their squads, they discover the stars carrying the flag for the diaspora in North America.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi in India colours as BCCI names squad for tri-series The Indian Cricket Board has named the India A squad for tri-series against Sri Lanka A and Afghanistan A. Tilak Varma has been named as the captain of the 15-member squad.

Nahid Rana crowned ICC Player of the Month for stunning New Zealand displays Bangladesh pacer Nahid Rana won the ICC Men's Player of the Month award after spearheading Bangladesh's ODI series victory over New Zealand.

Golden chance for Arshdeep Singh to find form: S Badrinath ahead of MI clash S. Badrinath challenged Arshdeep Singh to rediscover his best against Mumbai Indians, dismissing fatigue concerns and calling the PBKS pacer's form crucial in the race for the IPL playoffs.

Madonna, BTS and Shakira to headline first World Cup final halftime show FIFA has confirmed that Madonna, Shakira and BTS will headline the first-ever halftime show at the FIFA World Cup 2026 final at MetLife Stadium.

Thailand Open: PV Sindhu, Satwik-Chirag storm into quarter-finals PV Sindhu and the men's doubles pair of Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty advanced to the quarter-finals with commanding second-round wins at the Thailand Open.

Axar Patel, Rahane, Pant face IPL captaincy axe after disastrous campaigns Axar Patel, Rishabh Pant and Ajinkya Rahane could lose their captaincy after poor IPL campaigns, sources tracking franchise developments told PTI. The scrutiny has sharpened as playoff hopes fade and teams assess leadership, selections and coaching support.

Virat Kohli's century proves old guard still runs the show, says Sunil GavaskarSunil Gavaskar hailed Virat Kohli's unbeaten century against KKR as proof that the old guard still has plenty to offer, with the RCB star silencing doubters, smashing records and punishing KKR for an early dropped chance.

Pat Cummins slams rumours of leaving BBL for SA20 over payment disparityPat Cummins has rubbished reports suggesting Australian players may prioritise SA20 over the BBL from 2028, as the debate over franchise pay gaps and Cricket Australia's contract structure intensifies.

Hardik will leave MI after IPL 2026: Ex-India captain makes bold predictionKris Srikkanth has claimed Hardik Pandya will leave Mumbai Indians after IPL 2026. MI have struggled under Hardik's captaincy and suffered an early exit from IPL 2026.

After Sreejesh's fiery exit, Hockey India appoint Soyez as Jr.team coachHockey India appointed Frederic Soyez as coach of the junior men's team a day after PR Sreejesh's exit from the role. Soyez comes in with a big reputation, having worked as the head coach of Spain and France before.

If you drop Virat Kohli in a chase, bound to get difficult: Abhishek NayarRCB vs KKR: Abhishek Nayar admitted KKR paid the price for dropping Virat Kohli, while also pointing to Varun Chakravarthy's absence as a crucial factor in the six-wicket defeat to RCB.

Told you so: Krunal Pandya's prediction for Virat Kohli comes true in RaipurRCB vs KKR: Krunal Pandya's faith in Virat Kohli paid off spectacularly as the RCB star bounced back from back-to-back ducks with a match-winning century against KKR in Raipur. Kohli's stunning knock not only powered RCB to the top of the table but also shattered multiple T20 records.

PBKS vs MI, Dharamshala weather: Will rain spoil Punjab's playoff chances?Punjab Kings and Mumbai Indians head to Dharamsala with both sides short on momentum. For Punjab, the bigger concern is not just rain but a campaign wobbling at the wrong time.

PBKS vs MI: Punjab Kings can't stop the rumours. They seek to stop the rotPBKS vs MI, IPL 2026 preview: Punjab Kings have answered the noise on social media, backed their players and drawn a line. Now, at a rain-soaked Dharamsala, the only statement that actually matters is the one they make on the field against Mumbai Indians on Thursday.

City smash Palace to keep pressure on Arsenal in Premier League title raceManchester City breezed past Crystal Palace at home to keep pressure on Arsenal in the Premier League title race on Wednesday, May 13. City cut the gap to Arsenal to two points.

Virat Kohli's double act: When the human and the superhuman showed up in RaipurIPL 2026, RCB vs KKR: After back-to-back ducks, Virat Kohli walked out in Raipur nervous, celebrated a single like it was a six, and then scored a match-winning hundred. One night, two sides of the most compelling cricketer of his generation were on full display.

IPL Play of the Day: Kohli, Padikkal return as RCB's perfect chase comboRCB vs KKR: Whenever Virat Kohli and Devdutt Padikkal settle into an IPL 2026 chase together, panic slowly disappears from the RCB dugout. One controls the tempo, the other destroys the pressure, and Bengaluru suddenly make big run chases look far easier than they should.

Virat Kohli's special night lights up Raipur with plethora of records: In StatsIPL 2026, RCB vs KKR: Virat Kohli smashed a sensational century and shattered multiple records as Royal Challengers Bengaluru climbed to the top of the table with a six-wicket win over Kolkata Knight Riders on Wednesday in Raipur.

Virat Kohli no longer chasing records, just soaking in cricket before it endsIPL 2026: Virat Kohli struck an unbeaten 105 as RCB beat KKR by six wickets in Raipur. After reaching multiple milestones, he said he now wants to savour every day left in cricket.

Catch of the season? Manish Pandey leaves Virat Kohli, KKR teammates stunnedIPL 2026: Manish Pandey pulled off a one-handed diving catch to dismiss Tim David during RCB's chase in Raipur. Virat Kohli's century and RCB's six-wicket win still left KKR facing a tougher qualification path.

Virat Kohli roars back in landmark clash to dent Kolkata's playoffs hopes badlyIPL 2026, RCB vs KKR: Virat Kohli had a dream day as he scored a hundred and completed 14000 T20 runs as Royal Challengers Bengaluru beat the Kolkata Knight Riders by six wickets on Wednesday in Raipur.

CSK's playoff-push at risk after Jamie Overton flies back to UK due to injuryIPL 2026: Jamie Overton will return to the UK after suffering a right thigh injury. His exit leaves Chennai Super Kings without a key all-round option in a crucial playoff phase.

Virat Kohli ends duck streak with animated celebration in RCB vs KKRIPL 2026: Virat Kohli ended his run of ducks with an animated first-run celebration against Kolkata Knight Riders in Raipur. The moment capped his record 279th IPL appearance and eased the focus on his brief slump.

Why did Varun Chakravarthy miss the game vs RCB? Head coach Abhishek Nayyar revealsIPL 2026: Abhishek Nayyar said Varun Chakravarthy missed KKR's game against RCB Iin Raipur after suffering a hairline inury. The injury is on his landing foot, leaving the franchise hopeful rather than certain about his return next match.

Sarah Taylor breaks new ground as England men's first-ever female coachSarah Taylor has become the first woman to join the England men's coaching setup, adding another historic milestone to her trailblazing cricket journey after successful stints with Sussex, Team Abu Dhabi, England Lions and Gujarat Giants.

Real Madrid lose again! This time in court over homophobic chants against GuardiolaCAS upheld UEFA's sanctions after Real Madrid challenged punishment over homophobic chants aimed at Pep Guardiola. The ruling strengthens football's harder line on discrimination and leaves Madrid with another off-field defeat.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
